promotion_rule: Update this registry first, then regenerate reports and rerun validators before widening autonomy scope.

prerequisites.label: DEV governor-facade cutover
notes: The retired :8760 governor facade is gone from implementation authority and the 2026-03-29 collector shows zero observed runtime references.
Use the generated migration report and cutover packet as audit evidence, not as a forward blocker.

prerequisites.label: Provider routing truth
notes: Ordinary auto-routing now derives from canonical routing policy, provider catalog, and subscription-burn registry instead of hardcoded scheduler or CLI tables.
Configured-unused and handoff-only providers are demoted out of ordinary auto-routing until observed evidence exists.

prerequisites.label: VAULT LiteLLM provider auth repair
notes: The first software-core phase can run on currently proven local and cloud lanes, but broader API-backed autonomy should not widen until the auth-failed Vault providers are repaired.
The generated packet already enumerates the exact missing env names and re-probe sequence.

prerequisites.label: Runtime ownership maturity
notes: Broader full-system autonomy still depends on tightening DEV and VAULT runtime ownership so host-level mutations and maintenance work stop relying on implied operator memory.

approval_gates.label: Runtime mutations
blocked_actions: live service restarts outside an approved maintenance packet
VAULT LiteLLM config or env changes

approval_gates.label: Provider scope
blocked_actions: treating auth-failed or configured-unused providers as ordinary auto-routing lanes
treating governed-handoff-only providers as ordinary auto-routing lanes without explicit opt-in

phases.label: Software-core autonomy
allowed_loop_families: provider routing and governed fallback selection
self-maintenance inside repo and task-engine bounds
blocked_without_approval: new provider activation outside catalog-backed posture
entry_criteria: The DEV governor-facade cutover remains verified.
Canonical provider routing posture remains catalog-backed.
Operator presence and release-tier posture remain live.
success_criteria: Governed autonomous work can plan, route, execute, and persist results across proven local and cloud lanes.
No runtime mutation occurs without explicit approval.
Status, reports, and route surfaces all describe the same activation scope.

phases.label: Expanded core autonomy
blocked_without_approval: VAULT provider expansion without repaired auth posture
entry_criteria: The VAULT LiteLLM auth-repair packet is complete for the currently blocked API lanes.
Weak CLI and billing lanes have either been verified or explicitly kept demoted.
success_criteria: The broader live roster can execute within the same governed provider and approval boundaries as software-core autonomy.

phases.label: Full-system autonomy
blocked_without_approval: runtime mutations that still exceed the standing approval gate
entry_criteria: Runtime ownership is explicit enough that host-level maintenance no longer depends on undocumented operator memory.
Creative and refusal-sensitive loops stay on sovereign-only lanes by contract.
success_criteria: Athanor can operate across the full live roster under registry-backed scope, provider posture, and approval boundaries.
